About the Company:

Our client is a global organisation with a range of iconic brands that has been regarded as an "Employer of Choice" in their industry. This Finance Business Partner is a newly created role which will involve analysing the business, forecasting financial performance, generating & providing actionable commercial insights, and using influencing skills to drive profitable and sustainable growth for ANZ. 

Key Responsibilities:

Act as the "go-to" Finance Business Partner for an inspirational GM; specifically across Sales, Marketing and Commercial to provide commercial insights and analysis.
Supporting Marketing campaigns and promotional activity through analysis; ensuring ROI and profitability.
Lead the 5-year long range planning process and annual budget cycle
Deliver Net Revenue, Operating Income, and Working Capital targets
Directly manage, coach and mentor a junior Analyst; helping to foster their professional growth
Assist in leading the Integrated Business Planning (IBP) process
Assist in leading the Sales & Operations Planning (S&OP) process
Delivering variance analysis, KPI performance reporting, and margin management.
Supporting new business cases, pricing decisions, and commercial initiatives.
Proactively identify and present-on trends that will impact the company's financial position
Conduct scenario analysis to evaluate commercial risks and opportunities
Ad-hoc project work that arises + continually having a focus on process improvement and overall enhancement
The successful applicant will have 
Ideally, you will be a CA/CPA qualified accounting professional and will possess excellent communication and interpersonal skills as your ability to interact and engage cross-functionally within the business will be crucial to your success.
You will ideally have a career background in a "tangible-product" environment (i.e. FMCG / Retail / Pharmaceutical/ etc.). Furthermore, you will be passionate around collaborative business partnering and working with stakeholders. Prior hands-on experience with Power BI will also be considered advantageous. 

Additional Benefits

International travel opportunities; global mobilisation is encouraged if employees are passionate to do so
Work for a Global "Employer of Choice," and an industry-leader in their sector
Free car parking available 
High visibility and impact role – your insights will truly shape decisions
Hybrid working arrangements - 3 days in office and 2 from home.
First-class training programs & support schemes
How to Apply:
To apply, please click on the appropriate link below. Alternatively, for a confidential discussion, please contact Jack Billingham at jbillingham@sharpandcarter.com.au.

Optus is an Australian telecommunications company, delivering more than 11 million services to our customers every day across mobile, broadband and digital solutions.

As a Senior Analyst, Corporate Strategy & Development you will have an opportunity to create a significant impact on our company's strategic direction. You will support driving comprehensive analysis and insights to define our long-term strategic planning and growth.

What You'll Do | Your day to day will consist of

Develop financial models and quantitative analyses underpinning strategic modules of work.

Synthesise analysis into clear strategic narratives, insights and recommendations for senior management.

Monitor and report on market/industry trends and competitive dynamics to identify emerging threats and opportunities to support strategic decision making.

Support the development and delivery of Optus' strategy through objective research, analysis, and evaluation.

Prepare board-level strategy papers, presentations and other discussion documents and be able to communicate findings in clear, compelling formats.

Build effective working relationships across the organisation.

What You Bring | What makes you perfect for the role?

Tertiary qualifications in Finance, Economics, Business, Commerce, or a related field.

Approximately 4-6 years of professional experience in strategy, corporate development, management consulting, or in-house corporate strategy/finance functions.

Advanced financial modelling and valuation skills combined with strong commercial competence, backed by proficiency in Powerpoint/Excel and ability to develop well-structured strategic narratives.

Experience working with strategy in ambiguous and fast-paced environments.

Outstanding written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to tailor messages to various stakeholders.

Proven track record of delivering high-quality analytical work for senior stakeholders across the organisation.

Strategic outlook with the ability to think creatively and propose innovative solutions.

Collaborative individual with excellent interpersonal abilities and a talent for working effectively across departments.

The good stuff…. | What's in it for you?

Competitive remuneration and colleague discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and outstanding shopping discounts with our retail partners.

Flexible working arrangements with opportunities to work three days in the office, two days remote or home.

Vibrant and collaborative office campus that includes cafes, convenience store, chill out zones, GP, post office, gym and on-site childcare centre.

Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.

We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.

Direct your own development and build future-ready skills with Optus U's industry-focused micro-credentials, developed with La Trobe and Macquarie Universities.

Build meaningful connections through colleague-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).

Put your wellbeing first with free access to counselling and support services, 24/7 — in-person, by phone, SMS, or video.

Free Optus bus from Macquarie University Metro Station (every 6-8 minutes); morning and afternoon.

DXC Technology (NYSE: DXC) is a leading enterprise technology and innovation partner delivering software, services, and solutions to global enterprises and public sector organizations — helping them harness AI to drive outcomes at a time of exponential change with speed. Learn more on dxc.com
 
We are currently looking for an experienced SAP Integration Senior Consultant – PI/PO to join our high performing team in Canberra or Melbourne.  Candidates must hold NV1 clearance.

In this role you will be involved in a variety of integration activities mainly focused across PO and CPI, working on a range of exciting projects.  Your outstanding communication skills will allow you to develop strong customer relationships and operate at a high level within our consulting environment.

To be successful you will require the following;

Strong SAP PI/PO required
Strong understanding of integration patterns, concepts and standards across enterprise applications
SAP AIF and CPI / Cloud Integration Suite experience advantageous
Experience in ABAP would be highly beneficial
Strong stakeholder engagement skills
Excellent interpersonal skills
Previous experience working in a consulting environment, working across either projects or BAU 
NV1 or above is mandatory
 
In alignment with our commitment to fostering a flexible and dynamic work environment, we have adopted a hybrid working approach that acknowledges the importance of in-person collaboration while recognising that the determination of your work location will be based on a range of factors to ensure both individual preferences and operational requirements are met.

If you would like to be part of a culture that drives innovation, delivers results, rewards performance, and encourages ideas, then please press the "Apply Now" button to submit your resume.

The Opportunity

This is a standout and newly created opportunity for an ambitious Development Analyst to join a rapidly growing property group with a strong pipeline of projects and significant capital backing.

The Development Analyst will deliver financial modelling, analysis, and commercial insight across the development lifecycle - from acquisition through to delivery and stabilisation.

Working closely with development and finance teams, you will maintain feasibility models, manage cashflow and funding analysis, and monitor project performance across a live portfolio.

The Role

As a key member of the finance and development team, you will provide analytical support across the development lifecycle post-acquisition, partnering closely with Development Managers to deliver accurate financial modelling and project insights.

Key responsibilities include:
 
Building and maintaining development feasibility models across multiple projects
Analysing key metrics including IRR, NPV, development margin, and return on cost
Managing project cashflows, funding requirements, and drawdown analysis
Supporting lender reporting and funding processes
Monitoring project performance vs feasibility, identifying risks and opportunities
Undertaking market research, pricing analysis, and competitor benchmarking
Contributing to Investment Committee and Board materials
Playing a key role in improving modelling, reporting, and systems as the business scales

About You
 
2–5 years’ experience in property development, property finance, valuation, or investment analysis
Advanced Excel and financial modelling capability
Strong understanding of development feasibility and key investment metrics
Exposure to residential, land, or multi-stage developments highly regarded
Commercially astute with strong attention to detail
Confident communicator, able to engage with both technical and non-technical stakeholders

Please contact Adrian de la Nougerede on 0401403101 / adrian@capstonerecruitment.com.au
